title: Logic Gates and Logic Circuits
summary: 9618 AS — Boolean logic, truth tables, logic gates, and circuit design with GeoGebra logic gates explorer.
learningObjectives: Draw and interpret NOT, AND, OR, NAND, NOR, XOR gates.
Construct truth tables for combinational circuits.
Apply Boolean algebra and De Morgan's laws.
Build simple circuits from a given truth table.

simpleExplanation.title: Digital decisions
simpleExplanation.summary: Logic gates implement Boolean operations on binary inputs. Combining gates builds adders, multiplexers, and memory elements inside the CPU.
steps: NOT inverts; AND outputs 1 only if all inputs 1; OR if any input 1.
NAND/NOR are universal — any logic function can be built from either alone.
De Morgan: NOT(A AND B) = NOT A OR NOT B.
From truth table: identify minterms, simplify, draw gate diagram.

sections.content: **Logic circuits** appear on every 9618 Paper 1. Use the **interactive gate builder** below, then practise **truth tables** and **Boolean simplification** on past papers.

items: XOR: 1 when inputs differ — used in half-adders
Half-adder: Sum = A XOR B; Carry = A AND B
SR flip-flop: two cross-coupled NOR gates store 1 bit
